quick_skim.title: Bounding the Null Space: Interval-Based Uncertainty Quantification for Non-Identifiable Groundwater Models
quick_skim.abstract: Groundwater models are routinely non-identifiable: sparse subsurface observations leave many combinations of parameters, states, and boundary conditions equally consistent with the available data. Existing uncertainty quantification (UQ) methods address this by exploring a finite set of model realizations, but incomplete exploration can systematically underestimate the true range of admissible solutions. We propose a fundamentally different approach based on Optimization-based Bound Tightening (OBBT), which represents uncertainty directly as intervals and tightens them by extremizing variables over a constraint system encoding physical laws and observations. This yields guaranteed outer bounds on all uncertain variables without sampling, side-stepping the exploration problem entirely. To apply OBBT to groundwater flow, we discretize Darcy's law using a finite-volume scheme and handle the resulting bilinear terms through McCormick relaxations. We show that these relaxations can break the sign coupling between fluxes and head gradients, permitting non-physical rotational flow and failing to provide sufficient information for effective bound tightening. We identify flow sign prescription and irrotationality constraints as effective remedies and characterize their respective strengths and limitations. We demonstrate the framework on three numerical examples - a 1D steady-state model, a 2D steady-state model across four experimental configurations, and a 2D transient model on a hexagonal grid - and discuss computational performance, scalability, and directions for future research. OBBT offers a conservative, deterministic, and physically grounded alternative to ensemble-based UQ, with natural connections to null space theory and data assimilation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: interval-based uncertainty quantification for non-identifiable groundwater models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于区间的不确定性量化方法用于非唯一性地下水模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于区间的不确定性量化方法用于非唯一性地下水模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于区间的不确定性量化方法用于非唯一性地下水模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an interval-based uncertainty quantification approach for non-identifiable groundwater models, yielding guaranteed outer bounds by extremizing variables over a constraint system encoding physics and observations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对地下水模型普遍存在的非唯一性问题，提出一种基于优化界限紧缩的区间不确定性量化方法，将不确定性表示为区间并在物理定律与观测约束下极值化变量以紧缩界限，最终获得所有不确定变量的保证外限，从而避免传统有限实现探索可能导致的系统性低估，为数据匮乏条件下地下水模拟的可靠不确定性传播提供了新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对地下水模型普遍存在的非唯一性问题，提出一种基于优化界限紧缩的区间不确定性量化方法，将不确定性表示为区间并在物理定律与观测约束下极值化变量以紧缩界限，最终获得所有不确定变量的保证外限，从而避免传统有限实现探索可能导致的系统性低估，为数据匮乏条件下地下水模拟的可靠不确定性传播提供了新工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 海岸带地下水及数值模拟相关研究, especially work that connects or combines: coastal aquifer hydrogeology and water resources; seawater intrusion monitoring and management; porewater flow and chemical exchange across sediment-water interfaces; Numerical simulation methods for groundwater flow and transport; variable-density flow and solute transport simulation; characterization of coastal aquifers using geophysical methods; Recent developments in submarine groundwater discharge research and quantification methods; Assessing saltwater intrusion vulnerability under sea level rise scenarios; Mapping offshore fresh groundwater reserves and their resource potential; What are the best practices for modeling saltwater intrusion with density dependent flow?.
